title: Piquant Korean Fish Jjigae
description: A spicy, cheese‑pull Korean stew featuring tender white fish fillets and tandoori‑spiced broth, FODMAP‑friendly.
instructions: Heat garlic‑infused oil in a pot over medium heat until fragrant.
Add the tandoori spice mix, grated ginger, and black pepper, stirring for 30 seconds.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring to a gentle simmer.
Stir in gochujang, soy sauce, and fish sauce until fully blended.
Add sliced carrot and zucchini, cooking for 3 minutes until they soften slightly.
Gently fold in the fish fillet pieces, ensuring they are submerged in the broth.
Cover and simmer for 6 minutes until the fish is opaque and cooked through.
Sprinkle the shredded cheddar cheese over the stew, letting it melt into a gooey layer.
Remove from heat, garnish with chopped green onion tops, and give a quick stir.
Serve hot, spooning the cheese‑rich broth with the fish into bowls.
